title: Why I Stopped Recommending Alternatives to Payment Methods for Filipino Digital Creators: What You Need to Know
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: Selling digital products from Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, or Bangladesh is possible with crypto-native infrastructure that has no geographic restrictions.

content.text: I used to recommend alternatives to payment methods for digital creators in the Philippines, but I no longer do. The reasons are simple: they often come with restrictions, high fees, or complicated setup processes that hinder creators' ability to focus on producing content. One major obstacle is the lack of accessible payment options for international buyers, making it challenging for Filipino creators to reach a broader audience. The solution lies in adopting blockchain-based payment systems, which offer greater flexibility and accessibility.
The Honest Reality Most Creators Avoid

Despite the abundance of payment alternatives, most creators remain unaware of the true costs associated with these options. Some popular alternatives, like PayPal, charge high fees for international transactions, while others, like Stripe, require a bank account to onboard buyers. These restrictions limit creators' potential to grow their audience and increase their earnings. By acknowledging these challenges, we can begin to explore more effective solutions.
Why the Popular Advice Is Wrong

I used to advise creators to use alternatives like Payoneer or TransferWise, but these options often come with hefty fees and complex setup processes. Moreover, the majority of these alternatives require a bank account, which many Filipino creators do not have. This leaves them with limited options for receiving payments. I realized that the best advice is not to provide alternatives, but to equip creators with a more efficient and accessible solution: blockchain-based payments.
What the Data and Experience Actually Show

Data from the Philippine e-commerce market reveals that the most significant obstacle for creators is the lack of accessible payment options for international buyers. This restricts creators' ability to reach a broader audience and grow their earnings. By adopting blockchain-based payments, creators can tap into a global market, increasing their potential earnings and reach. This is not just a theoretical benefit; numerous creators have successfully used blockchain-based payments to expand their audience and increase their revenue.
Blockchain-based payments offer greater flexibility and accessibility for digital creators in the Philippines.
The Uncomfortable Truth About Your Options

The truth is, most payment alternatives for digital creators in the Philippines are limited and restrictive. This forces creators to rely on manual payment processing, which can be time-consuming and prone to errors. By acknowledging this reality, we can begin to explore more efficient solutions that empower creators to focus on producing high-quality content rather than worrying about payment processing.
What You Should Do Based on All of This

The solution lies in adopting blockchain-based payment systems, which offer greater flexibility and accessibility. With Web3 Wallet Connect, creators can integrate multi-chain QR codes and live price feeds into their digital products, allowing buyers to pay in a variety of cryptocurrencies. This not only expands the creator's audience but also reduces transaction fees and increases earnings potential. By making the switch to blockchain-based payments, Filipino creators can overcome the limitations of traditional payment alternatives and tap into a global market.
